subtopics.title: The Elements of Music
common_misconceptions: Rhythm and beat are the same
Melody and harmony are the same
subtopics.teaching_note: Pitch: highness or lowness of sound. Melody: organized sequence of pitches (tune). Harmony: simultaneous combination of sounds (chords). Rhythm: pattern of long and short sounds. Tempo: speed of music (allegro=fast, adagio=slow). Dynamics: loudness/softness (forte=loud, piano=soft). Timbre: tone colour/quality that distinguishes instruments. Texture: how layers of sound relate (monophonic, polyphonic, homophonic). Form: structure of musical piece (binary, ternary, rondo, sonata).

subtopics.title: Traditional Nigerian Music
subtopics.teaching_note: Characteristics of African music: polyrhythm (multiple rhythms simultaneously), call and response, communal participation, oral transmission, connection to dance. Yoruba music: Sakara, Apala, Juju (King Sunny Ade, Ebenezer Obey), Fuji (Kollington Ayinla, Wasiu Ayinde). Igbo music: Highlife (Osita Osadebe, Oliver De Coque), Egwu Ekpili, Ogene. Hausa music: Hausa classical, Waka (Barmani Choge), modern Hausa pop. Afrobeat: Fela Kuti (created), Femi Kuti, Seun Kuti, Burna Boy (modern afro-fusion). Musical instruments: membranophones (drums), idiophones (bells, rattles), chordophones (strings), aerophones (flutes, horns).
